# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.

Write secure code.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI Release Workflow
on: [push]
jobs: 
    build-and-publish:   # job name, you could use any valid GitHub Action Job Name here (e.g., 'build', etc.)    
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest      
        
        steps:         
            - uses: actions/checkout@v2  # Check out your repository using the latest version of Actions  
            
            - name: Setup NodeJS environment   
              uses: actions/setup-node@v1    
              with:     
                node-version: '14'      
                
            - run: npm ci  # Install dependencies (if any)        
              
            - run: npm run build -- if you have a different command to generate your production bundle, use that instead.   
            
        name: Publish Release  
          runs-on: ubuntu-latest    
          
          steps:     
              # You need the following permissions in order to publish on PyPI and npm  (e.g., `write-access` token)      
            - uses: actions/github-npm-package@v1  
            
        env:   
                NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}     # Needed to get PRs and other info from Actions     
        
          with: 
              package_dir: '.'
